calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FOX TELEVISION STATIONS, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Fox Television Stations, LLC's special motion to strike Plaintiff Alfredo Rahmaan Balderramos's first amended complaint (FAC) under the anti-SLAPP statute is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Continued To Jun-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FOX TELEVISION STATIONS, LLC, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS KTVU FOX 2's MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Continued on court's own motion to June 11, 2026.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DANIEL LURIE , MAYOR OF SAN FRANCISCO, AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT

Appearances (by Zoom):
Alfredo Rahmaan Balderramos 415-9965161 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Tara Steeley, Esq. 415-554-4655 Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.
David Brons, Esq. 510-343-5839 Kronenberg Law PC for Defendant Islamic Center of San Francisco.
Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
Mr. Balderramos contests and presents arguments; homeless, death threats, met with the mayor but ignored, impromptu strike, 50 minutes in front of mosque, protested peacefully, factual disputes, minimum merit met, mayor's office wants 15K, drop everything, disabled.
Court comments.
Ms. Steeley submits to the TR, Plaintiff needs to show evidence to prove case.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Daniel Lurie's Special Motion to Strike Plaintiff Alfredo Rahmaan Balderramos's complaint under the Anti-SLAPP Statute is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Reported. (302/JMQ)
